rationale: The citing paper explicitly positions its contribution as extending prior dyadic trust/governance work to a multiparty platform context, and the cited paper is a direct dyadic governance study on buyer–supplier transaction structure (contracts vs JVs) and its determinants/consequences. That makes the citation plausibly a foundational/theory source rather than a clearly incidental mention. With only DOI-level matched evidence and no in-text snippet, this is not safe to drop.
